significant_events.event_name: Animal Birth Control and Anti-Rabies Vaccination Programme (ABC/ARV)
significant_events.source_quote: Blue Cross of Hyderabad currently focuses on the Animal Birth Control and Anti Rabies Vaccination Programme (ABC/ARV) in collaboration with Greater Hyderabad Municipal Corporation (GHMC).
significant_events.summary: Systematic implementation of ABC/ARV programs across multiple municipalities in collaboration with GHMC to control stray animal population and prevent rabies.

significant_events.source_quote: More than 4000 dogs were vaccinated across the city.
significant_events.summary: A large vaccination drive in association with Mission Rabies and WVS, vaccinating more than 4000 dogs across the city to achieve a Rabies Free Telangana.

significant_events.event_name: World Rabies Prevention Day Vaccination Camp (September 28, 2024)
significant_events.source_quote: Blue Cross of Hyderabad conducted a successful vaccination camp, where an impressive 4,415 dogs were vaccinated with the support of WVS and Mission Rabies over a fortnight leading to the event.
significant_events.summary: A successful vaccination camp where 4,415 dogs were vaccinated with support from WVS and Mission Rabies, raising awareness about rabies prevention.

significant_events.source_quote: A large-scale community geo-tagging, for the first time in Hyderabad and Anti-Rabies Vaccination drive was conducted in Manikonda, covering over 812 street dogs.
significant_events.summary: A large-scale community geo-tagging and Anti-Rabies Vaccination drive covering over 812 street dogs in Manikonda, creating a traceable and protected dog population.

significant_events.source_quote: A 5-month ABC & Vaccination project sponsored by Infosys, was led by CSR team- Vamshi Muthyapu & Padmaja in Pocharam region. 500 dogs were covered in areas of Korremulla and Chowdariguda.
significant_events.summary: A 5-month ABC & Vaccination project sponsored by Infosys, covering 500 dogs in Pocharam region, demonstrating a valuable corporate partnership.

significant_events.source_quote: Blue Cross of Hyderabad organized a vaccination camp, successfully vaccinating 355 dogs.
significant_events.summary: A vaccination camp successfully vaccinating 355 dogs, raising awareness about zoonotic diseases and ensuring animal and community health.

significant_events.source_quote: Blue Cross of Hyderabad and Worldwide Veterinary Service conducted specialized ABC surgery training for two batches of Government Vets, training 16 veterinarians in modern welfare practices for street dogs.
significant_events.summary: Specialized ABC surgery training for two batches of Government Vets, training 16 veterinarians in modern welfare practices for street dogs, strengthening grassroots veterinary care.

significant_events.source_quote: The Blue Cross team travelled to Goa for further training under WVS. They gained hands-on experience in welfare interventions. The team included Mrs. Kumari (COO), Shekhar, Upendhar, Naresh, and Uday Kiran. Here the team worked as resource trainers and shared their field experiences.
significant_events.summary: Blue Cross team travelled to Goa for further training under WVS, gaining hands-on experience in welfare interventions and sharing field experiences as resource trainers.

significant_events.source_quote: The ABC team of Blue cross of Hyderabad was selected for WVS advanced training in Mumbai.The program focused on vaccination and field skills enhancement with geo tagging.
significant_events.summary: The ABC team of Blue Cross of Hyderabad was selected for WVS advanced training in Mumbai, focusing on vaccination and field skills enhancement with geo tagging.

significant_events.source_quote: Blue Cross of Hyderabad begins operations under the Toopran Project initiative. Marking the launch with the signing of the MOU, dogs were caught for ABC/ ARV. This project is aimed to address street animal population and welfare, and marked a significant step toward expanding humane interventions for the same.
significant_events.summary: Blue Cross of Hyderabad began operations under the Toopran Project initiative, marking a significant step toward expanding humane interventions for street animal population and welfare.

significant_events.source_quote: Launching the second project in Shankarpally municipality, Blue Cross of Hyderabad conducted sterilization, vaccination, and outreach work, broadening our footprint in suburban areas that need attention.
significant_events.summary: Launching the second project in Shankarpally municipality, conducting sterilization, vaccination, and outreach work, broadening the footprint in suburban areas.

significant_events.source_quote: Blue Cross of Hyderabad launched a new Animal Birth Control (ABC) project at the Cantonment area on December 1st, 2024.
significant_events.summary: Blue Cross of Hyderabad launched a new Animal Birth Control (ABC) project at the Cantonment area, aiming to humanely manage the street dog population through sterilization and vaccination.

significant_events.event_name: School Awareness Programs on Animal Welfare (Narsingi Government Schools)
significant_events.source_quote: We conducted awareness sessions in schools to educate students on responsible pet care, rabies prevention, and the importance of animal birth control.
significant_events.summary: Awareness sessions in schools to educate students on responsible pet care, rabies prevention, and the importance of animal birth control, fostering compassion.

significant_events.event_name: Awareness Camp at Gokaraju Rangaraju College
significant_events.source_quote: Blue Cross of Hyderabad conducted an awareness camp at Gokaraju Rangaraju College, focusing on key issues such as animal welfare, zoonotic diseases, vaccinations, and the ABC/ARV programs.
significant_events.summary: An awareness camp focusing on animal welfare, zoonotic diseases, vaccinations, and ABC/ARV programs, emphasizing responsible pet ownership and disease prevention.

significant_events.event_name: Secunderabad Awareness Drive & Water Bowl Distribution
significant_events.source_quote: As part of our community outreach initiative, Blue Cross of Hyderabad conducted a series of awareness programs across the Secunderabad region. The goal was to sensitize the public on animal welfare issues, especially during the summer months when street animals struggle to find clean drinking water.
significant_events.summary: Community outreach initiative to sensitize the public on animal welfare issues and distribute water bowls to ensure street animals have access to drinking water.

significant_events.source_quote: Adoptions are organized on a dedicated Adoption Day held on the last Sunday of every month, helping to find loving homes for these young animals and reducing the number of strays on streets.
significant_events.summary: Monthly adoption days held to find loving homes for puppies and kittens cared for at the shelter, reducing the number of strays.

significant_events.source_quote: Our much awaited annual event celebrating native Indian dogs, organized by Blue Cross of Hyderabad, hosted by Chaitanya Vidyalaya and prizes sponsored by MARS Petcare. It drew enthusiastic participation with over 70 entries from pet parents, reaffirming the growing recognition and appreciation for these resilient and loyal dogs.
significant_events.summary: An annual event celebrating native Indian dogs, promoting adoption and responsible pet ownership with over 70 entries from pet parents.

significant_events.source_quote: Radio Mirchi teamed up with Blue Cross of Hyderabad for a large-scale adoption drive drawing attention to shelter animals who need homes. The event connected adopters with well-cared-for vaccinated pets and prompted a host of timely adoptions.
significant_events.summary: A large-scale adoption drive in partnership with Radio Mirchi, connecting adopters with well-cared-for vaccinated pets and prompting timely adoptions.

significant_events.source_quote: Blue Cross of Hyderabad received the award for service in animal welfare. This recognition highlighted the team's impactful community work, boosting morale and strengthening trust in the mission.
significant_events.summary: Blue Cross of Hyderabad received an award for service in animal welfare, recognizing the team's impactful community work.

data.original_text: Blue Cross of Hyderabad is a non-profit organization dedicated to street animal welfare.
Their mission is to focus on animal birth control, vaccinations, and animal welfare training.
They work in alliance with the GHMC to implement the Animal Birth Control Anti-Rabies Vaccination program for stray dogs in specific areas of the city.
The organization also provides basic checkups, vaccinations, and emergency care for sick and injured animals.
They offer training for volunteers, veterinarians, pet owners, and animal welfare groups.
Adoption events are held monthly to facilitate the adoption of healthy puppies and kittens.
The organization does not accept abandoned animals or house pets for boarding and encourages the use of government veterinary hospitals and GHMC animal shelters.
To support their cause, visitors can donate, sponsor, volunteer, or become a member through their official website.
Located in Hyderabad, Telangana, the Blue Cross of Hyderabad operates from a two-acre animal shelter in Jubilee Hills.
The shelter is surrounded by hills and vast open spaces, housing over 650 animals of different species.

The organisation prevents cruelty to animals, rescues the sick and the injured, shelters them, provides mobile clinics, educates people on animal welfare and carries out many more animal welfare activities that upholds the existence of these simple souls.
To reduce the number of homeless stray animals in the streets, Blue Cross vaccinates over 15,000 stray dogs, against rabies, each year.
The organisation sterilises around 600 street dogs and cats every month and vaccinates them against rabies.
It also conducts extensive adoption drives to find homes for stray puppies and kittens to reduce the number of homeless strays in the streets.
Many who semi own dogs but cannot afford Veterinary treatment, become allies in the dog management of the area.

Blue Cross aims to reach a large target audience within the community so they support the Animal Birth Control/Anti-Rabies Vaccination efforts, how to behave around animals and thereby create safe communities.
Our Animal Welfare Training Centre facilitates training for Veterinarians, pet owners, animal welfare groups and students.
We conduct an effective Animal Birth Control/ Anti Rabies Vaccination program for stray dogs in West Zone, Jubilee and Banjara Hills, 15 kms around our location.
In 2016, BCH trained 114 government vets from Andhra Pradesh, organising a three-week training program-me that was conducted in six batches.

The annual report summarizes the activities of Blue Cross of Hyderabad for 2019-2020. It highlights that 11,409 animals were helped over the year, including 9,708 that underwent animal birth control operations.

During the financial year of 2018-19, Blue Cross of Hyderabad was able to provide help to 10,844 animals; of which 8198 were through the Animal Birth Control /Anti Rabies Vaccination programme, and the rest were treated in emergency care, at our clinic and through our adoption drives.
Our effort proved to be extremely successful to reduce stray dog population on the street and thereby reduce animal suffering exponentially.

We proved this by actually seeing a reduction in animals in accidents, less puppies dying of parvo and distemper, and more people helping animals; while ABC operated dogs are being accepted by their communities in the areas we work.
This mature and responsible attitude has boosted the number of people and groups helping animals to a new record.
Since inception through services and efforts, Blue Cross of Hyderabad has helped over 5,35,790 animals.

The pandemic year was completely handled by Mrs.kumari , our CFO and Our Veterinarians Dr.Shubam, Dr.Haritha, Dr.Irshad, supported by their teams of dedicated staff. We stayed focused on Animal Birth Control and Rabies control efforts.
The impressive numbers of animals helped, 38,640 to be precise, are due to a well coordinated team effort established over the years.
To highlight the work of our ambulance and rescue service, 8500 animals were rescued, sick, injured and a bused from the streets.
